palette_hint: Warm cream background #FDF6EC, dark espresso text #2C1A0E, with burnt-orange accent #C1440E and muted olive green #6B7A3E for section dividers and highlights.
type_style: Headings in Georgia serif at bold weight for a handwritten-cookbook warmth; body text in system-ui sans-serif at 1rem/1.6 line-height for clean readability.
description: Copper Kettle Recipes is a single-dish deep-dive site dedicated to a classic Hungarian Beef Goulash, walking visitors through every detail from ingredients to the cook's personal story.
constraints: All content centered in a single column, max-width 640px, with auto left/right margins.
Page padding is 24px top/bottom and 16px left/right on the content wrapper.
Section breaks use a thin 1px horizontal rule in color #C1440E with 32px vertical margin above and below.
Accent color #C1440E used only for headings, the site title, and the hr elements — never as a background fill.
Buttons (e.g. navigation links styled as buttons) are solid #2C1A0E background with #FDF6EC text, 12px 24px padding, no border-radius, displayed as block elements centered with a max-width of 200px.

page_specs.featured-recipe: Displays the recipe title 'Hungarian Beef Goulash' in a large Georgia h1 in #C1440E, followed by a two-sentence evocative description of the dish's smoky paprika aroma and tender braised beef. Below that, a metadata block lists prep time (20 min), cook time (2 hr 30 min), servings (6), and difficulty (Intermediate) each on its own line with a bold label, separated from the hero text by an hr divider.
page_specs.ingredients: Lists all ingredients under a bold Georgia h2 heading 'What You'll Need', organized into two labeled groups — 'For the Goulash' and 'For Serving' — each group introduced by a small olive-green #6B7A3E subheading. Ingredients are presented as a plain unstyled list with each item on its own line, e.g. '700g beef chuck, cut into 4cm cubes', '3 tbsp sweet Hungarian paprika', '1 green pepper, diced'.
page_specs.instructions: Presents numbered step-by-step cooking directions under the h2 'How to Make It', with each step as a list item beginning with a bold action word like 'Sear', 'Sweat', 'Simmer'. Steps cover browning the beef in lard, building the paprika base with onions and garlic, deglazing with beef stock, and the final low-and-slow braise; each step is 1–2 sentences of concrete instruction.
page_specs.tips-and-notes: Offers three focused tips under the h2 'Cook's Notes', each introduced by a bold italic title such as 'Paprika Quality Matters', 'Don't Rush the Simmer', and 'Make It Ahead'. Each tip is a short paragraph of 2–3 sentences with specific, practical advice like using Pride of Szeged sweet paprika and never letting the goulash boil after the paprika is added.
page_specs.about-the-cook: Introduces the fictional recipe author Marta Vásárhelyi in a short 3–4 sentence biography explaining she grew up in Debrecen, Hungary, learned this goulash from her grandmother, and now lives in Toronto where she teaches weekend cooking workshops. Ends with a plain-text contact invite: 'Questions? Write to marta@copperkettlerecipes.com' styled in the body font without a hyperlink.
